metadata.name: hw2004_t7_rho10_senior_mezz_6_10
metadata.description: Hull-White (2004) Table 7 benchmark: [6,10]% tranche on a 100-name homogeneous pool at flat Gaussian correlation 0.1.
metadata.source_detail: Hull, J. and White, A. (2004), 'Valuation of a CDO and an nth to Default CDS Without Monte Carlo Simulation', Journal of Derivatives 12(2), Table 7. Fixture: 100-name homogeneous pool, flat hazard 1%/yr (Act/Act ISDA), recovery 40%, flat 5% continuously-compounded risk-free rate (Act/360), 5Y quarterly premium (2006-09-01 to 2011-09-01, Act/360, Following), Gaussian copula at flat correlation. Expected outputs are the published breakeven running spreads in bp (whole-bp table resolution); cross-referenced against QuantLib test-suite/cdo.cpp hwData7, which validates QuantLib's own CDO engines against the same table at 1-10bp absolute / 4-7% relative tolerance.
metadata.regen_command: manual transcription of the published Table 7 values; see source_detail

plan.description: Static Hull-White Table 7 market snapshot: flat 5% cc Act/360 discounting (log-linear DF knots reproduce exp(-0.05t) exactly), flat 1% Act/Act-ISDA hazard, flat base-correlation curve. No calibration steps; curves are carried in prior_market.

par_spread.tolerance_reason: HW publishes whole-bp spreads (+/-0.5bp table rounding). Band covers that rounding plus the documented convention residual between finstack's tranche engine (EL at payment dates, (1-f)*dEL accrual-on-default, exact conditional binomial at n=100) and HW's mid-period premium-accrual recursion. Measured residual on 2026-07-22: ours 89.86bp vs published 89bp. QuantLib's own engines carry 1-10bp abs / 4-7% rel bands against this table.

dv01.tolerance_reason: Internal deterministic regression pin, NOT a Hull-White value (the paper publishes only fair spreads). Computed by finstack's own dv01 calculator on this analytic fixture on 2026-07-22 and pinned at ~10x FP-residual tightness so refactor drift is caught; the external benchmark assertion for this fixture is par_spread.

cs01.tolerance_reason: Internal deterministic regression pin, NOT a Hull-White value (the paper publishes only fair spreads). Computed by finstack's own cs01 calculator on this analytic fixture on 2026-07-22 and pinned at ~10x FP-residual tightness so refactor drift is caught; the external benchmark assertion for this fixture is par_spread.
